title: South Korea Pro: ROK refiners draw down strategic petroleum reserves on Iran blockade
summary: South Korea Pro reports that South Korean refiners have begun drawing down strategic petroleum stocks while accelerating Saudi and UAE crude lifting in response to Brent crude's spike to $126 per barrel and continued US naval blockade of Iranian ports.
contents:
# South Korea Pro: ROK refiners draw down strategic petroleum reserves on Iran blockade

## ROK Industry Response
South Korea Pro reporting on April 30 details the response by South Korean refining companies to the Brent crude price spike to $126 per barrel and the ongoing US naval blockade of Iranian ports. Refiners reportedly began drawing down strategic petroleum stockpiles while simultaneously accelerating crude lifting from Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates to replace lost Iranian and adjacent shadow-fleet supply.

## Government Coordination
ROK Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy and the Ministry of National Defense reportedly held coordination meetings with refiners and shipping operators to ensure continuity of crude supply for both civil and military purposes. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs has reportedly engaged Washington on the secondary-sanctions implications of the newly-designated Chinese refinery, given South Korea's downstream linkages with Chinese intermediaries.

## Strategic Implications
South Korea Pro assesses that prolonged blockade enforcement could strain South Korean refining margins and accelerate the search for long-term diversification away from Middle East crude exposure. The episode reinforces the case for continued ROK investment in Indo-Pacific maritime security cooperation, including the trilateral US-Japan-ROK framework and bilateral cooperation with Australia and the Philippines.
